definition: a baby dog
example: The little puppy wagged its tail and licked my hand.

definition: a baby cat
example: The soft kitten curled up in my lap and purred.

definition: a small rabbit
example: The white bunny hopped across the grass.

definition: a baby duck
example: The yellow duckling followed its mother to the pond.

definition: a very young bird, especially a baby chicken
example: The fluffy chick pecked at the seeds on the ground.

definition: a baby sheep
example: The little lamb stayed close to its mother in the field.

definition: a baby bear, lion, or fox
example: The bear cub played near the river while its mother watched.

definition: a baby deer
example: The spotted fawn hid in the tall grass.

definition: a baby horse
example: The long-legged foal ran beside its mother.

definition: a small horse
example: She got to ride a pony at the farm.

definition: a male chicken that crows
example: The rooster crowed loudly every morning at sunrise.

definition: a female chicken that lays eggs
example: The hen sat on her eggs to keep them warm.

definition: a big bird that honks and lives near water
example: The goose honked and flapped its wings at us.

definition: a large bird with a long neck that lives on water
example: The beautiful swan glided across the lake.

definition: a bird that hunts at night
example: The owl hooted from the tree when it got dark.

definition: a small bird with a red or orange chest
example: A robin sat on the fence singing a sweet song.

definition: a small brown bird
example: The tiny sparrow pecked crumbs from the sidewalk.

definition: a big black bird
example: The crow cawed loudly from the top of the tree.

definition: a very large bird that flies high
example: The eagle soared high above the mountains.

definition: a very large sea animal that breathes air
example: We saw a huge whale jump out of the ocean.

definition: a smart sea animal that swims and breathes air
example: The dolphin leaped out of the water and splashed back in.

definition: a large ocean animal with sharp teeth
example: The shark swam fast through the dark water.

definition: a reptile with a hard shell that covers its body
example: The turtle pulled its head inside its shell.

definition: a small animal like a snake but with four legs and a long tail
example: A green lizard sat on the warm rock in the sun.

definition: a small animal that jumps and lives near water
example: The frog jumped off the lily pad into the pond.

definition: a small bumpy animal that hops and lives on land
example: The toad sat in the mud near the garden.

definition: a tiny animal with a shell that moves slowly
example: The snail left a shiny trail on the leaf.

definition: a tiny soft animal with no legs that lives in dirt
example: After the rain, a worm came out of the dirt.

definition: a small animal with eight legs that makes webs
example: The spider made a web between two branches.

definition: an insect with a hard shell
example: A shiny black beetle crawled across the log.

definition: a small red bug with black spots
example: A ladybug landed on my finger, and then flew away.

definition: an insect with big colorful wings
example: The orange butterfly flew from flower to flower.

definition: a small animal with many legs that turns into a butterfly
example: The green caterpillar munched on leaves all day.

definition: a tiny bug that lives in a group
example: The ant carried a crumb back to its hill.

definition: a small flying insect that buzzes and can sting
example: The bee buzzed around the yellow flowers.

definition: a small animal with a bushy tail that climbs trees and collects nuts
example: The squirrel hid a nut inside the tree hole.

definition: an animal with black fur around its eyes like a mask
example: A raccoon knocked over the trash can at night.

definition: a black and white animal that smells bad
example: We smelled the skunk before we even saw it.

definition: an animal that builds dams with sticks
example: The beaver chewed on a log to build its home.

definition: a very big deer with large flat antlers
example: The huge moose stood in the middle of the road.

definition: bread that is cooked until brown
example: I put butter on my toast for breakfast.

definition: food you eat with milk, often for breakfast
example: He poured cereal into his bowl and added milk.

definition: a flat round food you eat for breakfast
example: Dad made a pancake shaped like a smiley face.

definition: a breakfast food with little squares on it
example: I poured syrup into the squares of my waffle.

definition: a hot breakfast food made from oats
example: On cold mornings, I like a warm bowl of oatmeal.

definition: food made with bread and something inside
example: Mom packed a peanut butter sandwich in my lunch box.

definition: a salty snack made of twisted bread
example: I ate a warm pretzel with mustard.

definition: thin dry food that crunches
example: She put cheese on each cracker.

definition: long thin food made from dough
example: He slurped the long noodle into his mouth.

definition: a small soft cake
example: The blueberry muffin was still warm from the oven.

definition: a small cake with frosting on top
example: She licked the pink frosting off her cupcake.

definition: a small sweet flat food
example: Grandma baked chocolate chip cookies for us.

definition: round sweet food with a hole
example: The doughnut was covered in sugar and sprinkles.

definition: a soft sweet food you eat with a spoon
example: The chocolate pudding was cold and smooth.

definition: a soft sweet spread for bread
example: I like grape jelly on my toast.

definition: a thick sweet stuff you pour on food
example: He poured syrup all over his pancakes.

definition: sweet food that bees make
example: Bears love to eat honey from beehives.

definition: corn that pops when you heat it
example: We ate popcorn while we watched the movie.

definition: thick creamy food made from milk
example: He stirred the yogurt and added some berries on top.

definition: a small round fruit that grows in bunches
example: She picked a purple grape off the vine.

definition: a small round red fruit
example: The cherry on top of the ice cream was bright red.

definition: a soft fruit with fuzzy skin
example: Juice dripped down my chin when I bit the peach.

definition: a small purple fruit
example: The ripe plum was sweet and juicy.

definition: a large fruit with a thick skin and soft inside
example: We cut the melon into slices on a hot day.

definition: a small juicy fruit
example: We picked every berry from the bush in our yard.

definition: a yellow fruit that tastes sharp and not sweet
example: The lemon was too sour to eat by itself.

definition: a big brown fruit with white inside
example: We cracked open the coconut and drank the water inside.

definition: a small food that grows in a shell underground
example: The elephant ate a peanut right from my hand.

definition: a long green crunchy vegetable
example: She dipped the celery stick into peanut butter.

definition: a green vegetable that looks like a tiny tree
example: The broccoli on my plate looked like little trees.

definition: green leaves you eat in a salad
example: The rabbit munched on the fresh green lettuce.

definition: a crunchy vegetable that can be red, green, or yellow and is hollow inside
example: Mom sliced a red bell pepper for our salad.

definition: a round vegetable that makes you cry
example: Cutting the onion made tears run down my face.

definition: a living thing with a cap on top and a stem, that grows in damp places
example: The mushroom grew under the big tree after the rain.

definition: a thick hot meal of meat and vegetables cooked slowly in a pot
example: The warm stew was perfect on a cold winter night.

definition: a thick sauce you pour on meat
example: He poured gravy all over his mashed potatoes.

definition: a very big special meal
example: The family had a feast with turkey and pie.

definition: a small bit of food between meals
example: After school, he had a snack of apples and cheese.

definition: something special that makes you happy
example: Ice cream after dinner was a special treat.

definition: a thin piece cut from something
example: Can I have one slice of pizza, please?

definition: the part in the middle of your arm that can bend
example: She bumped her elbow on the table and said ouch.
imageKeyword: arm with arrow pointing to elbow joint

definition: where your hand meets your arm
example: She wore a pretty bracelet on her wrist.
imageKeyword: hand and arm with arrow pointing to wrist

definition: where your foot meets your leg
example: He twisted his ankle when he jumped off the step.
imageKeyword: foot and leg with arrow pointing to ankle

definition: the back part of your foot
example: Her shoe rubbed a blister on her heel.
imageKeyword: foot with arrow pointing to heel

definition: the short fat finger on your hand
example: The baby sucked his thumb when he was sleepy.

definition: the flat inside part of your hand
example: He held the coin in the palm of his hand.

definition: a hand with all fingers closed tight
example: He made a fist and knocked on the door.

definition: the bottom part of your face
example: Juice dripped down his chin when he ate the peach.
imageKeyword: face with arrow pointing to chin

definition: the soft side of your face
example: Grandma kissed him on the cheek.

definition: the flat part above your eyes
example: Mom felt my forehead to see if I had a fever.
imageKeyword: face with arrow pointing to forehead

definition: the line of hair above your eye
example: He raised one eyebrow when he was surprised.

definition: tiny hairs on the edge of your eye
example: An eyelash fell on her cheek, and she made a wish.

definition: the soft part inside your mouth
example: The dog stuck out its tongue and licked my face.

definition: the inside of your neck where food and air pass
example: My throat hurt so much I could not swallow.

definition: where your arm connects to your body
example: She carried the bag over her shoulder.
imageKeyword: person with arrow pointing to shoulder

definition: the wide part where your legs meet your body
example: He put his hands on his hips and said stop.
imageKeyword: person with arrow pointing to hip

definition: the bones down your back that help you stand straight
example: Stand up straight, so your spine is not curved.
imageKeyword: back with arrow pointing to spine

definition: the bones around your chest that protect your body
example: He laughed so hard his ribs hurt.

definition: the bone that covers your brain
example: Helmets protect your skull if you fall.

definition: the part of your body that helps you move
example: He flexed his arm to show his muscles.

definition: a warm cover for your hand with no fingers
example: She put on a fuzzy mitten to keep her hand warm.

definition: a long cloth you wrap around your neck
example: The red scarf kept her neck warm in the snow.

definition: a shirt with a hood on the back
example: He pulled the hood of his hoodie over his head in the rain.

definition: a jacket with no sleeves
example: He wore a warm vest over his shirt.

definition: cloth you wear to keep clean while cooking
example: Mom tied her apron before she started baking.

definition: the part of a shirt that covers your arm
example: He rolled up his sleeve before washing his hands.

definition: a small bag sewn into clothes
example: He put the rock in his pocket to save for later.

definition: something that opens and closes your jacket
example: He pulled up the zipper on his coat because it was cold.

definition: a small round thing that holds clothes closed
example: One button on his shirt popped off.

definition: a metal clip that holds a belt closed
example: He snapped the buckle on his belt.

definition: the string you tie on a shoe
example: She pulled the lace tight and tied a bow on her shoe.

definition: a soft shoe you wear inside
example: She put on her fuzzy slippers when she got out of bed.

definition: an open shoe for warm weather
example: He wore sandals to the beach so his feet could breathe.

definition: a shoe for running and playing
example: She put on her sneakers before going to the playground.

definition: a tall shoe that covers your ankle
example: He wore rain boots so his feet stayed dry.

definition: the part of a shirt around your neck
example: The tag on his collar was itchy.

definition: the bottom edge of a skirt or pants
example: The hem of her dress dragged on the floor.

definition: clothes you wear to bed
example: She changed into her pajamas and brushed her teeth.

definition: special clothes to look like someone else
example: He wore a pirate costume for Halloween.

definition: the same clothes everyone in a group wears
example: All the players wore the same team uniform.

definition: a soft cover for your bed
example: She pulled the warm blanket up to her chin.

definition: a soft thing you rest your head on
example: He fluffed his pillow before going to sleep.

definition: cloth you use to dry off
example: She wrapped a towel around herself after her bath.

definition: what you use to get clean
example: The soap made lots of bubbles in the bath.

definition: a soft thing that soaks up water
example: Dad wiped the table with a wet sponge.

definition: a long stick with stiff hairs at the end, used for sweeping
example: She used the broom to sweep the crumbs off the floor.

definition: a round open thing with a handle for carrying water or sand
example: He filled the bucket with sand at the beach.

definition: something with steps you climb up
example: Dad used a ladder to reach the roof.

definition: a box that slides in and out of a desk
example: She put her socks in the top drawer.

definition: a flat board on a wall for holding things
example: The books sat in a row on the shelf.

definition: a small room where you keep clothes
example: He hung his coat in the closet.

definition: cloth that covers a window
example: She opened the curtain to let the sun in.

definition: a soft cloth on the floor
example: The cat slept on the soft rug by the fire.

definition: something that gives light
example: He turned on the lamp to read his book.

definition: a stick of wax that burns with a flame
example: We lit a candle when the lights went out.

definition: a jar you put flowers in
example: She put the roses in a pretty vase.

definition: a border around a picture
example: The photo was in a wooden frame on the wall.

definition: a paper cover for a letter
example: She sealed the envelope and put a stamp on it.

definition: a small sticker you put on mail
example: You need a stamp to send a letter.

definition: a box that is sent to you
example: A big package came in the mail today.

definition: a tool for cutting paper
example: She used scissors to cut out the paper star.

definition: sticky stuff you use to stick things together
example: He used glue to stick the picture on the poster.

definition: sticky strip for holding things
example: He used tape to hang the drawing on the fridge.

definition: a colored stick for drawing
example: She picked a red crayon to color the apple.

definition: a soft stick for writing on a board
example: He drew a rainbow on the sidewalk with chalk.

definition: something you rub to erase pencil marks
example: She rubbed the eraser over her mistake.

definition: a straight tool for measuring
example: He used a ruler to draw a straight line.

definition: a tool that tells how hot or cold it is
example: Mom put the thermometer in my mouth to check my temperature.

definition: the thing that gives power to a toy
example: The toy stopped working because the battery ran out.

definition: the thing you flip to turn something on or off
example: He flipped the switch and the room went dark.

definition: a building on a farm for animals
example: The cows went into the big red barn at night.

definition: a building where horses live
example: The horse walked back into its stable after the ride.

definition: a small house made of wood
example: We stayed in a cozy cabin in the mountains.

definition: a small pretty house
example: The old woman lived in a little cottage by the woods.

definition: a big stone building where kings live
example: The princess lived in a tall castle on the hill.

definition: a tall thin building
example: She looked out from the top of the tower.

definition: a road over water or a valley
example: We walked across the bridge to get to the other side of the river.

definition: a road that goes through a mountain
example: The train went into the dark tunnel.

definition: a safe place where boats park
example: The boats were tied up in the harbor.

definition: land with water all around it
example: The island was so small you could walk around it in one hour.

definition: a big area full of trees
example: They got lost in the dark forest.

definition: a big open field of grass and flowers
example: The deer ate grass in the sunny meadow.

definition: a small body of water
example: The ducks swam around the pond.

definition: a small river
example: We stepped on rocks to cross the stream.

definition: a very tall rock wall
example: The bird nested on the high cliff.

definition: a hole in a rock or mountain
example: The bear went into the dark cave to sleep.

definition: a very dry place with very little rain
example: Nothing grew in the hot desert except cactus.

definition: a thick forest with many plants
example: Monkeys swung from tree to tree in the jungle.

definition: wet muddy land with water
example: The alligator hid in the dark swamp.

definition: low land between two mountains
example: The little town sat in the green valley.

definition: very bad weather with wind and rain
example: The storm blew the tree branches everywhere.

definition: the loud boom sound in a storm
example: The thunder was so loud it shook the windows.

definition: a flash of light in the sky during a storm
example: The lightning lit up the whole sky for a second.

definition: a curved band of colors in the sky after rain
example: After the rain stopped, a beautiful rainbow appeared.

definition: a gentle soft wind
example: The light breeze made the leaves dance.

definition: thin ice that forms on cold mornings
example: The grass was white with frost on the cold morning.

definition: a long pointed piece of ice hanging down
example: A long icicle hung from the roof.

definition: a small pool of water on the ground
example: She jumped in every puddle on the way to school.

definition: wet dirt
example: The pig rolled in the mud to stay cool.

definition: tiny bits of dry dirt in the air
example: The old book was covered in dust.

definition: tiny water drops on grass in the morning
example: The dew on the flowers sparkled in the sun.

definition: a cloud close to the ground
example: The fog was so thick we could not see the road.

definition: balls of ice that fall from the sky
example: The hail bounced off the car like little white stones.

definition: a heavy snow storm with strong wind
example: The blizzard dropped so much snow we could not open the door.

definition: a long time with no rain
example: The flowers turned brown because of the long drought.

definition: water covering land that is usually dry
example: The river rose so high it caused a flood.

definition: one soft piece of a flower
example: A pink petal fell from the rose.

definition: the long green part of a plant
example: He held the flower by its long green stem.

definition: the part of a plant under the ground
example: The tree's root went deep into the dirt.

definition: a sharp point on a plant
example: She pricked her finger on the rose's thorn.

definition: a plant that grows along the ground or up walls
example: The vine climbed all the way up the old wall.

definition: a soft green plant that grows on rocks and trees
example: The rocks were covered in soft green moss.

definition: a nut that falls from an oak tree
example: The squirrel buried the acorn to eat later.

definition: the brown seed holder from a pine tree
example: She picked up a pinecone from under the big tree.

definition: a tiny thing that grows into a plant
example: She planted a seed and watered it every day.

definition: to move on your hands and knees
example: The baby learned to crawl before he could walk.

definition: to jump far
example: The frog leaped across the pond.

definition: to move with little hops and steps
example: She liked to skip down the path singing a song.

definition: to step down very hard
example: He would stomp his feet when he was angry.

definition: to walk very quietly on your toes
example: She had to tiptoe past the sleeping baby.
